_meta.name: Kali Agent Vulnerability Knowledge Base — Seed Data
_meta.description: Pre-loaded intel for the vuln-knowledge-base artifact. Import via storage API or paste into the KB manually.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: F5 BIG-IP APM Remote Code Execution
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.remediation: Patch to fixed BIG-IP version per F5 advisory K000156741
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: Pre-auth RCE when APM access policy is configured on a virtual server. Reclassified from DoS to RCE in March 2026.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: Cisco Firewall Management Center Deserialization RCE
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: Unauthenticated deserialization in web-based management interface allows arbitrary Java code execution as root.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: Google Dawn Use-After-Free (Chromium)
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.remediation: Update Chrome to latest stable channel
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: Use-after-free in Dawn allows RCE via crafted HTML page. Affects all Chromium-based browsers.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: Zimbra ZCS Remote File Inclusion
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: PHP remote file inclusion via /h/rest endpoint without authentication.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: Versa Concerto Authentication Bypass
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: Authentication bypass allows attacker to access administrative endpoints.

cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.title: Langflow AI Code Execution
cisa_kev_critical_2025_2026.description: Code execution vulnerability in AI workflow platform.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Broken Access Control (incl. SSRF, IDOR, BOLA)
owasp_top10_2025.description: #1 risk category. 18.4% of all web findings at Cobalt are XSS (subset). Includes SSRF (previously separate category). Test: role-based access, direct object references, forced browsing, API authorization.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Implement server-side access control, deny by default, use ABAC/RBAC, log access failures, rate limit APIs.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Security Misconfiguration
owasp_top10_2025.description: Jumped to #2. Includes missing security headers, verbose errors, default credentials, unnecessary features enabled, cloud misconfig (S3 buckets, IAM).
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Harden configurations, remove defaults, automate config scanning, implement IaC security scanning.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Software Supply Chain Failures
owasp_top10_2025.description: Expanded from 'Vulnerable Components'. Includes dependency confusion, typosquatting, compromised build pipelines, malicious packages.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Use SCA tools, pin dependency versions, verify package integrity, scan CI/CD pipelines.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Insecure Design
owasp_top10_2025.description: Expanded to cover AI-integrated attack surfaces: model injection, prompt manipulation, trust boundary failures in LLM-calling apps.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Threat modeling before development, secure design patterns, abuse case testing.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Injection (SQLi, XSS, Command, SSTI)
owasp_top10_2025.description: XSS = 18.4% of all web findings. SQLi = 10.6%. Combined = 29% of all web pentest findings (Cobalt 2025 data). Still the most commonly found issues despite OWASP demotion.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Parameterized queries, output encoding, input validation, WAF, CSP headers.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Cryptographic Failures
owasp_top10_2025.description: Weak TLS, cleartext data transmission, weak hashing (MD5/SHA1), hardcoded keys.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Use TLS 1.2+, strong hashing (bcrypt/argon2), proper key management.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Identification & Authentication Failures
owasp_top10_2025.description: Weak passwords, missing MFA, session fixation, credential stuffing susceptibility.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Implement MFA, use strong password policies, secure session management.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Software & Data Integrity Failures (incl. CI/CD)
owasp_top10_2025.description: Now includes CI/CD pipeline attacks. Unsigned updates, insecure deserialization, compromised build systems.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Code signing, integrity verification, secure CI/CD, review deserialization.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Logging & Alerting Failures
owasp_top10_2025.description: Average breach discovery time ~200 days. Missing audit trails, no alerting on auth failures.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Centralized logging, SIEM integration, alert on suspicious activity.

owasp_top10_2025.title: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions (NEW)
owasp_top10_2025.description: Brand new category. Covers 24 CWEs: verbose error messages leaking paths/stack traces, exceptions bypassing security checks, race conditions in security functions.
owasp_top10_2025.remediation: Fail closed, generic error messages, catch all exceptions in security paths, test error paths.

common_pentest_findings.title: Default Admin Credentials
common_pentest_findings.description: admin/admin, admin/password, root/root on web panels, databases, network devices. Found in ~15% of external pentests.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Change all default credentials, enforce password policies, use credential vaults.

common_pentest_findings.title: Exposed Admin Panel on Internet
common_pentest_findings.description: /admin, /wp-admin, /phpmyadmin, /manager accessible without IP restriction. Often the initial access vector.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: IP whitelist admin interfaces, use VPN, implement MFA on admin panels.

common_pentest_findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Auth Endpoints
common_pentest_findings.description: Login forms and API auth endpoints without rate limiting allow credential stuffing and brute force.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting, account lockout, CAPTCHA after N failures.

common_pentest_findings.title: Outdated WordPress Plugins
common_pentest_findings.description: WordPress plugins with known CVEs remain the #1 web CMS attack vector. wpscan typically finds 3-5 vulnerable plugins per site.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Update all plugins, remove unused plugins, use managed WordPress hosting.

common_pentest_findings.title: IDOR in API Endpoints
common_pentest_findings.description: Changing user IDs in API requests (/api/user/123 → /api/user/124) exposes other users' data. #1 bug bounty finding category.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Server-side authorization checks on every request, use UUIDs instead of sequential IDs.

common_pentest_findings.title: JWT Secret Weakness
common_pentest_findings.description: JWT signed with weak/default secret (e.g., 'secret', 'password'), allowing token forgery.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Use strong random secrets (256+ bits), consider asymmetric signing (RS256).

common_pentest_findings.title: Missing Security Headers
common_pentest_findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options. Found on ~70% of tested sites.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Configure all security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy.

common_pentest_findings.title: Verbose Error Messages / Stack Traces
common_pentest_findings.description: Application returns full stack traces, database errors with column names, internal paths. Enables targeted attacks.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Custom error pages, disable debug mode in production, log errors server-side only.

common_pentest_findings.title: CORS Misconfiguration
common_pentest_findings.description: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or reflects arbitrary origin. Enables cross-site data theft.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Whitelist specific trusted origins, never reflect arbitrary origins, validate credentials flag.

common_pentest_findings.title: Subdomain Takeover
common_pentest_findings.description: CNAME pointing to deprovisioned cloud resource (S3, Azure, Heroku). Attacker can claim the resource and serve content.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Audit DNS records, remove stale CNAMEs, use DNS monitoring.

common_pentest_findings.title: Server Version Disclosure
common_pentest_findings.description: Server: Apache/2.4.52, X-Powered-By: PHP/8.1.0 in HTTP headers reveals exact versions for targeted exploits.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Configure server to suppress version headers (ServerTokens Prod, expose_php=Off).

common_pentest_findings.title: Directory Listing Enabled
common_pentest_findings.description: Web server returns directory contents when no index file present. May expose sensitive files.
common_pentest_findings.remediation: Disable directory listing (Options -Indexes in Apache, autoindex off in nginx).

ad_common_findings.title: Kerberoastable Service Accounts with Weak Passwords
ad_common_findings.description: Service accounts with SPNs whose passwords crack in under 1 hour. Found in ~60% of AD environments.
ad_common_findings.remediation: Use gMSA for service accounts, 25+ char passwords, monitor Kerberos ticket requests.

ad_common_findings.title: ADCS ESC1 — Misconfigured Certificate Template
ad_common_findings.description: Certificate template allows low-priv users to request certs as Domain Admin via SAN specification. Direct path to DA.
ad_common_findings.remediation: Audit templates with certipy find -vulnerable, remove enrollment rights, disable SAN specification.

ad_common_findings.title: LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning Possible
ad_common_findings.description: LLMNR and NetBIOS enabled on network allows credential interception via Responder.
ad_common_findings.remediation: Disable LLMNR and NBT-NS via GPO, enable SMB signing, implement network segmentation.

ad_common_findings.title: Password Spray — Season+Year Pattern
ad_common_findings.description: 43% of cracked AD passwords follow Season+Year pattern (Summer2025!, Winter2026). 22% follow Company+digits.
ad_common_findings.remediation: Ban common patterns via Azure AD Password Protection or custom dictionaries, enforce MFA.

ad_common_findings.title: Unconstrained Delegation on Computer Accounts
ad_common_findings.description: Computer accounts with unconstrained delegation can capture TGTs from any authenticating user.
ad_common_findings.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation, use constrained delegation or RBCD instead.

ai_augmentation_prompts.recon_analysis: Analyze these nmap results. For each open port: 1) What service and version is running? 2) Are there known CVEs for this version? 3) What are the most likely attack vectors? 4) What should I enumerate next?
ai_augmentation_prompts.finding_chain:
I found these issues on the same application:
[list findings]
How could these be chained together for a more severe attack? What's the maximum impact an attacker could achieve?
ai_augmentation_prompts.waf_bypass: The target has a WAF blocking my [XSS/SQLi] payloads. Based on the error responses I'm seeing, suggest 5 bypass techniques specific to this WAF behavior.
ai_augmentation_prompts.privesc_analysis:
Here's my LinPEAS/WinPEAS output:
[paste output]
Identify the top 3 privilege escalation paths, ranked by reliability. For each, provide the exact commands.
ai_augmentation_prompts.report_generation: Convert these raw findings into professional penetration test report sections. For each finding include: executive summary (business impact), technical details with reproduction steps, evidence, CVSS 3.1 justification, remediation with code examples, and references.
ai_augmentation_prompts.exploit_chain: Given this technology stack [list stack], what multi-step attack chains could lead from initial access to full compromise? Consider both known CVEs and logic flaws.
